Problem description

As described [here](https://discourse.bokeh.org/t/callback-on-finished-rendering/12098/1), render requests may pile up if sent too often.

### Feature description

It would be handy to have a feedback from the server whether the plot has been rendered. This way, a new request might be sent only after the plot has been finished already.

### Potential alternatives

An obvious workaround is to test how many request your setup is able to process and throttle the requests manually according to that.
If you have several plots to be refreshed at the same time, you can speed things a bit up by using 

> from contextlib import ExitStack()
> with ExitStack() as stack:
>     mgrs = [stack.enter_context(fig_list[ii].hold(render=True)) for ii in range(len(fig_list))]
>     for plot in plot_list:
>         plot.data={"x": data["x"], "y": data["y"]}

This way, the plots get refreshed all at once insteat of one by one.
However, it still requires you to check how fast your setup is able to render the plots.

### Additional information
